# @vllnt/convex-permissions

> Role-based access control as a Convex component — typed, sandboxed, runtime-editable roles and grants keyed by opaque refs

`@vllnt/convex-permissions` is a publishable Convex component. It targets `convex@^1.36.1`.

It provides stored, runtime-editable role-based access control. The component owns two sandboxed
tables — `roles` (a role name → its grants) and `assignments` (an opaque `subjectRef` → a role,
with an optional opaque `scopeRef` for multi-tenancy). The consumer-facing `Permissions<TRole,
TAction>` client, generic over the host's role and action unions, exposes `defineRole`, `removeRole`,
`assign`, `revoke`, `check`, `require`, `rolesFor`, `permissionsFor`, and `listRoles`. Grants support
exact, prefix-wildcard (`doc.*`), and global (`*`) matching; checks are default-deny and `require`
throws `ConvexError<PermissionDenied>`. The component is agnostic on every axis — opaque refs, no auth
library, no domain model, no vendor — and runs identically on Convex Cloud and self-hosted backends.

## Docs

- [README](README.md): Project overview, installation, usage examples, architecture
- [API Reference](docs/API.md): Public class methods, return shapes, input validation
- [Changelog](CHANGELOG.md): Version history and release notes
- [Contributing](CONTRIBUTING.md): Development setup, validation commands, release workflow

## Examples

- [Example component wrapper](example/convex/example.ts): Host-app wrapper functions
- [Example tests](example/convex/example.test.ts): End-to-end usage coverage with `convex-test`
